basic_details.job_title: Allahabad High Court RO ARO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 543 Review Officer, Assistant Review Officer & Computer Assistant Posts
basic_details.organization_name: High Court of Judicature at Allahabad
basic_details.post_name: Review Officer (RO), Assistant Review Officer (ARO), Computer Assistant (CA)
basic_details.short_information: Allahabad High Court is recruiting 543 RO, ARO & Computer Assistant posts with Graduation + Computer qualification, last date 21 June 2026.

important_dates.admit_card_date: To be announced later through Public Notice on official website
important_dates.exam_date: To be announced later through Public Notice on official website
important_dates.stage-ii & stage-iii examination: To be announced after Stage-I result

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay non-refundable examination fee through online mode only via State Bank of India (SBI MOPS). Fee once deposited will not be refunded under any circumstances and shall not be adjusted in any way. Applicable service/processing charges over and above the examination fee are payable by the candidate to the concerned bank. Since one common exam is held for all posts, candidates have to pay only one fee as per their vertical category. | Note: SC/ST candidates of states other than Uttar Pradesh shall pay fee as prescribed for General/OBC category. Payment modes: SBI/Other Bank Net Banking (Rs. 5 + GST), SBI/Other Bank Debit Card (Free), Credit Card (0.80% + GST), UPI (Free).

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01.07.2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per U.P. Government guidelines for reserved category candidates. Candidate claiming age relaxation in more than one category will be entitled to only one relaxation whichever is more beneficial.

qualification.nationality: Indian Citizens; also Tibetan refugees settled before 01.01.1972 and persons of Indian origin migrated from specified countries with eligibility certificate from State Government.
qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor's Degree from a University established by law in India, or equivalent qualification recognized thereto.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of submission of the Online Application Form. Only Indian Citizens (and certain categories as specified) are eligible to apply. Candidates must apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

vacancy_details.Description: One Arm, One Leg, Both Legs, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims (1% reservation)
Remaining 1% for LD/VI/HI on rotation basis

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Remaining 1% for LD/VI/HI on rotation basis

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be placed in the pay matrix as per the 7th Central Pay Commission. In addition to the basic pay, candidates are entitled to other admissible allowances as per the rules of The Hon'ble High Court of Judicature at Allahabad. | Allahabad High Court RO ARO Salary 2026

selection_process: Sl. Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration

1 General Science, History, Indian National Movement, Indian Polity/Economy/Culture, Agriculture/Commerce/Trade, Population/Ecology/Urbanisation, World Geography/Resources of India, Current Events, General Aptitude, UP Special Knowledge, General English & Hindi (Graduation Level), Elementary Computer Knowledge 200 MCQ 200 03 Hours (180 minutes)
A Essay Writing in English (approx. 200 words) 30 02 Hours (120 minutes)

syllabus.details: The examination consists of Three Stages. Stage-I is a screening test; Stage-II and Stage-III marks are used for final merit. All three stages must be individually qualified. The detailed syllabus for each stage is as under: | Stage-I — Preliminary Examination Syllabus (General Studies, 200 Marks, 200 MCQ, 3 Hours) | No Negative Marking. No Minimum Qualifying Marks. Question paper available in English and Hindi both; in case of any disparity, the English version shall be treated as final. | Stage-II — Mains Examination Syllabus (Subjective Type, 150 Marks, 2 Hours) | Stage-III — Computer Knowledge Test Syllabus (CBT Mode, 50 Marks, 20 Minutes) | Minimum Qualifying Marks: 25 out of 50. No Negative Marking for wrong answers; deductions apply for typing errors only (1 mistake = 0.1 marks deducted). Stage-II and Stage-III are conducted simultaneously in a single shift with a gap of 20 minutes on the same day at the same venue. | Allahabad High Court RO ARO Syllabus and Exam Pattern 2026

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted. Only one application is to be submitted by a candidate for the post of Review Officer / Assistant Review Officer / Computer Assistant. Candidates are advised to fill out the Online Application Form well in advance and not wait until the last date.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

Recent coloured passport-size photograph with applicant's name and date of capture printed on it (JPG/JPEG, 10 KB-200 KB); photo must show 80% face including both ears against white background - polaroid and computer-generated photos not acceptable
Signature in running handwriting with Blue/Black pen on white paper (JPG/JPEG, 10 KB-100 KB); capital letters not accepted
important_instructions: Candidates must appear in all three stages of the examination; it is mandatory to qualify each stage individually. No exemption from any stage shall be granted to any candidate including those belonging to the Physically Handicapped category. Final Merit List shall be prepared exclusively on the basis of aggregate marks in Stage-II and Stage-III. Candidates selected for RO post shall not be considered for ARO or CA; candidates selected for ARO shall not be considered for CA. Biometric information (Photograph, Thumb Impression and IRIS) of all candidates shall be captured at examination centres. Candidates must cooperate with security personnel for identity and security checks. The Provisional Answer Key for Stage-I shall be displayed on the official websites for 2-3 days, and candidates may challenge any answer key by paying Rs. 500/- per question challenged (refundable if challenge is accepted). No Score Card will be dispatched by post; candidates must download their Score Cards from the official websites after declaration of final result, as they will be available online for a limited period only. Mobile phones, Bluetooth devices, pagers, calculators, electronic watches with calculator facility or any other electronic gadget are strictly prohibited inside the examination premises. Possession of barred items will be treated as Unfair Means (UFM). Merely appearing and passing in the examination does not confer any right to the candidate for selection or appointment. Selection is subject to eligibility, rank in merit list, medical fitness and verification of original documents. Information uploaded on the official website shall not be provided under the RTI Act during the course of the recruitment process; factual information shall be provided only after declaration of the final result. All candidates already employed in Central/State Government or Public Undertakings must apply subject to production of NOC from their Cadre Controlling Authority as and when asked for. All disputes pertaining to the conduct of this examination shall fall within the jurisdiction of Allahabad only. The High Court reserves the right to alter the number of vacancies, modify the examination process or modify/alter the minimum cut-off marks without assigning any reason. Examination records will be retained for up to 90 days from the date of declaration of the final result. Canvassing in any form shall disqualify the candidature of the applicant. Candidates are bound by a Non-Disclosure Agreement upon registering. Sharing or reproducing any question paper content in part or whole through any medium is a punishable offence leading to cancellation of candidature.
